document.source_title: Big blow for MI! De Kock and Raj Angad Bawa ruled out of IPL 2026
document.summary: Mumbai Indians have faced a significant setback as both Quinton de Kock and Raj Angad Bawa have been ruled out for the remainder of IPL 2026 due to injuries. De Kock suffered a tendon injury in his left wrist, while Bawa sustained a ligament tear in his right thumb. The team, already out of playoff contention, confirmed that replacements for both players will be announced in accordance with IPL regulations. With two matches left, MI's disappointing season continues as they sit ninth on the points table.
key_takeaways: Quinton de Kock ruled out of IPL 2026 due to a tendon injury.
Raj Angad Bawa out with a ligament tear in his right thumb.
Mumbai Indians confirmed both players are recovering at home.
MI is already out of playoff contention, sitting ninth on the points table.
The team has two matches left to play this season.
Replacements for the injured players will be announced later.
document.why_it_matters: The injuries to key players like De Kock and Bawa highlight the challenges faced by the Mumbai Indians in IPL 2026, impacting their performance and strategy as they conclude a disappointing season.

claims.claim_text: Quinton de Kock is ruled out for the remainder of IPL 2026 due to a tendon injury.
claims.evidence_text: De Kock picked up a tendon injury in his left wrist ahead of Mumbai’s clash against Sunrisers Hyderabad.

claims.claim_text: Raj Angad Bawa is ruled out for the remainder of IPL 2026 due to a ligament tear.
claims.evidence_text: Bawa suffered a ligament tear in his right thumb during Mumbai’s previous fixture against Punjab Kings.

claims.claim_text: Mumbai Indians are out of playoff contention in IPL 2026.
claims.evidence_text: The five-time champions, who are already out of the playoff race, confirmed that both players are currently recovering at home.
